small.name: Typical Garden Apartment Complex
small.notes: 24-unit garden style; 2-3 stories; surface parking; laundry room; leasing office

medium.name: The Ayer
medium.notes: Midrise; 8 stories; 200 units; amenity deck; fitness; co-working lounge; structured parking

large.name: One Manhattan Square
large.notes: Highrise; 80 stories; 815 units; 100,000 SF amenity space; pool; spa; IMAX; garden

space_library.name: Residential Units (All)
notes: Aggregate of all residential unit types (Studio, 1BR, 2BR, 3BR); typically 55-75% of GSF

space_library.name: Main Lobby
notes: Include package visibility if feasible
Buffer to residential for acoustic/privacy

space_library.name: Vestibule / Airlock
notes: Useful for cold climates and IAQ
Coordinate door swings and ADA clearances

space_library.name: Mail + Package Room
notes: Design for delivery workflow
Consider smart lockers

space_library.name: Concierge / Security Desk
notes: Sightlines to entry, lobby, package/mail

space_library.name: Public Restroom (Single-User)
notes: Often omit in small buildings; add if leasing/amenities justify

space_library.name: Elevator Lobby (Typical Floor)
notes: Size increases with elevator count and corridor config

space_library.name: Stair (Resident)
notes: Coordinate egress, pressurization, and smoke control

space_library.name: Stair (Public/Entry)
notes: Only if public program requires dedicated stair access

space_library.name: Resident Corridor
notes: Gross impact is huge: single-loaded vs double-loaded drives efficiency

space_library.name: Unit Entry Zone (internal placeholder)
notes: Placeholder for adjacency anchoring to unit types

space_library.name: Trash + Recycling Room
notes: Locate for hauling; odor control; wash-down preferred

space_library.name: Trash Chute / Disposal Room
notes: Often near elevator lobby; consider fire-rated vestibule

space_library.name: Janitor Closet
notes: Mop sink; locate per floor or per wing

space_library.name: MEP Riser / Shaft Zone
notes: Aggregate risers centrally; align wet stacks where possible

space_library.name: Electrical Closet (Per Floor)
notes: Coordinate with utility company metering approach

space_library.name: Fire Sprinkler Riser Room
notes: Typically at ground/basement with FDC access

space_library.name: Main Mechanical Room
notes: Size depends on system type (central plant vs distributed)

space_library.name: Mechanical Room (Sub / Floor)
notes: Use where stacked PTAC/ERV/HRV equipment or hydronic distribution requires

space_library.name: Generator Room (Optional)
notes: Acoustics, ventilation, and code separation drive layout

space_library.name: Fuel Storage (Optional)
notes: Local code dependent; may be exterior tank

space_library.name: IT / Server Room (MDF)
notes: Dedicated cooling may be required

space_library.name: Loading / Dock (Optional)
notes: Often absent in small sites; replaced by curbside loading zone

space_library.name: Service Corridor (BOH)
notes: Useful for separating resident/public from trash/mechanical routes

space_library.name: Resident Lounge
notes: Often paired with kitchen/bar and outdoor terrace

space_library.name: Amenity Kitchen / Serving
notes: Not a commercial kitchen; confirm grease requirements

space_library.name: Fitness Room
notes: Higher ventilation requirement; consider acoustics to units

space_library.name: Co-Working / Work Lounge
notes: Acoustic separation from lounge; consider phone booths

space_library.name: Common Laundry Room (Optional)
notes: Energy/pressure impacts; consider out-of-envelope option in high performance projects

space_library.name: Parking Garage / Structured Parking
notes: Not always counted in building GSF; treat separately per pro forma

space_library.name: Bicycle Storage
notes: Consider e-bike charging, ramps vs stairs

space_library.name: Management Office / Leasing
notes: If leasing: place near lobby; if only management: can be BOH

space_library.name: Outdoor Terrace / Roof Deck
notes: Not in GSF typically; confirm with pro forma

space_library.name: Unit Kitchen (internal component)
notes: Component for deeper unit planning; not used in coarse programming
